name: Mirek Kaspar

name: Liberstake
description:
# Market Making, Cross-Chain Arbitrage & Managed Staking

## Connecting Liquidity. Stabilizing Markets. Generating Real Returns.

Liberstake is a crypto-native trading and infrastructure company specializing in market making, cross-chain arbitrage, and managed staking services. We don't speculate — we profit by making markets more efficient, and everyone benefits.

## What We Do

### Market Making

We provide deep, consistent liquidity to token pairs across decentralized and centralized exchanges. The result: tighter spreads, lower slippage, and healthier order books for projects and their communities. Whether you're a new token looking to bootstrap liquidity or an established protocol that needs reliable volume, we keep your markets running smoothly.

### Cross-Chain Arbitrage

Price discrepancies across chains and venues are inevitable. We close those gaps — fast. Our proprietary trading infrastructure identifies and executes profitable arbitrage opportunities across ecosystems, stabilizing prices and connecting fragmented liquidity in the process. Every trade we make leaves the market in a better state than we found it.

### Managed Staking

We operate and manage staking positions across proof-of-stake networks, delivering consistent yields without the operational overhead. Our clients earn passive returns while we handle validator selection, risk management, and reward optimization.

## Our Edge

- **Profitable by design** — every trade we execute is a profitable trade. We generate volume and stabilize prices not through subsidies or wash trading, but through genuine market efficiency
- **Cross-chain native** — we operate across EVM chains, Substrate networks, and major CEXs, bridging liquidity wherever it's fragmented
- **Infrastructure, not speculation** — we're the plumbing that makes markets work. Projects partner with us because we make their ecosystems more liquid, more stable, and more attractive to users and investors alike

## Our Roots

Liberstake was born in Liberland and proudly holds the distinction of being the **first company listed on the Liberland Stock Market** and the **first fully profitable, dividend-paying pure Liberland company**. Our DNA is rooted in the principles of free markets, transparency, and sovereign finance — and that ethos drives everything we build.

From a pioneering micro-nation to the global crypto markets, we've grown by doing one thing well: making markets work better.

---

📩 **Interested in market making, liquidity partnerships, or managed staking? Let's talk.** 

[Whatsapp](https://wa.me/+385923758682)

[liberstake@gmail.com](liberstake@gmail.com)

name: Stack4Me
description:
Stack4Me is a Startup for personalized supplement subscription that builds, delivers, and continuously optimizes a custom supplement stack tailored to your body, goals, and real-world results. You start with a detailed health and lifestyle questionnaire, and our algorithm designs a formula backed by peer-reviewed nutritional science. Every ingredient is selected for dosage, bioavailability, and how it interacts with the rest of your stack. You review it, tweak it if you want, and it ships to your door monthly.


Through daily check-ins in our app, you track energy, sleep, mood, and side effects — and every month, the algorithm uses that data to refine your stack. Ingredients that aren't delivering get swapped. Dosages get dialed in. What's working gets reinforced. The result is a supplement regimen that gets smarter the longer you use it.

name: ZChat Privacy Technologies
description:
ZChat Privacy Technologies is a software development company specializing in privacy-enhancing communication technologies. The company develops and maintains ZChat Messenger, an open-source application that uses Zcash shielded transactions to enable private, censorship-resistant messaging. Unlike traditional messengers requiring phone numbers or email addresses, ZChat allows users to communicate using only cryptographic wallet addresses, ensuring no personal data collection. The company's core product implements the ZMSG Protocol for encoding encrypted messages within blockchain transactions, providing mathematical guarantees of sender, receiver, and content privacy. Won a $600 prize at the Zypherpunk Hackathon 2025.

*Pulled from blockchain.liberland.org by Claude*

Registered in Liberland with EUR 10,000 initial capital.

Starting at the grassroots level by fostering human-to-human connections and establishing trust within local communities. The company's LuciVerse platform orchestrates autonomous AI agents across multiple tiers.

name: Furious Nerd Productions

Furious Nerd Productions is a film and television production company and publishing house based in Stewartstown, Pennsylvania. Evolved from "Nerd Rage News," a nerd-oriented news blog that ran for nearly a decade. Founder Steve Wollett has 150+ film and TV credits; films have won awards in the USA, UK, France, Germany, Russia, and India. Executive producers include award-winning actress Bai Ling and Australian actor Vernon Wells (The Road Warrior, Commando).

name: Pristine Water Sports
description:
Ocean adventure company at Pristine Bay. Guided snorkeling tours, fishing trips, sunset cruises, Cayos Cochinos day trips, nurse shark encounters, mangrove exploration, kayaking.

*Catalogued from prospera.co/en/marketplace by Claude*

name: Skyhawk Aviation
description:
Skyhawk Aviation is a pilot training school based at Gödöllő Airport near Budapest, Hungary. They offer modular EASA flight training courses — LAPL (Light Aircraft Pilot License), PPL (Private Pilot License), and NVFR (Night Vision Flight Rating) — using some of the market's safest and most modern aircraft equipped with Garmin glass cockpit avionics. Training is led by experienced instructors and the school welcomes international students. Owned by a Liberlander.

*Catalogued by Claude*

name: Priority Pass
description:
Priority Pass is the world's largest independent airport lounge access network, providing members entry to 1,800+ airport lounges and experiences in over 600 cities across 148 countries. Founded in 1992 and owned by the Collinson Group (UK). Offers three membership tiers — Standard, Standard Plus, and Prestige — with benefits including lounge access, dining, spa experiences, and airport services. Owned by a Liberlander.

*Catalogued by Claude*

name: Cryptospace Cards
description:
Cryptospace is a cryptocurrency services company headquartered in San Pedro, California. Their cards division offers cryptocurrency credit/debit cards — the "Cosmic Card" — designed to bridge the gap between cryptocurrency holdings and everyday spending. The company also operates across SaaS, media, NFT, and outsourcing divisions. Owned by a Liberlander.

*Catalogued by Claude*

name: Neti (Neti Sp. z o.o.)
description:
Neti is a Polish blockchain consulting and development company founded in 2010 in Rzeszów, Poland. Started as a software boutique specializing in JavaScript solutions and expanded into blockchain technology in 2019. Recognized as a Top 100 Blockchain Company for 2024–2025. Neti has partnered with Liberland and works across major blockchain ecosystems including Polkadot, Ethereum, Cosmos, Solana, and Hyperledger Fabric. Owned by a Liberlander.

*Catalogued by Claude*

name: Tomorrow Bio
description:
Europe's first cryonics company, providing emergency medical stabilization and long-term cryopreservation of the human body at -196°C immediately after legal death. Founded in 2020 by Dr. Emil Kendziorra and Fernando Azevedo Pinheiro. Collaborates with the European Biostasis Foundation in Rafz, Switzerland. Has cryopreserved 20 people and 10 pets, with 800+ signed up. Total contract value exceeds EUR 160M.

*Catalogued by Claude*

name: Alcor Life Extension Foundation
description:
The world's oldest and largest cryonics organization, founded in 1972. A 501(c)(3) nonprofit that preserves human bodies and brains in liquid nitrogen after legal death. Approximately 1,000 members and ~117 patients preserved. State-of-the-art facilities in Scottsdale, Arizona.

*Catalogued by Claude*

name: Cryonics Institute
description:
Nonprofit cooperative membership organization providing affordable cryonics services, founded April 4, 1976 by Robert Ettinger, "the Father of Cryonics." Owns its 7,000 sq ft facility in Clinton Township, Michigan outright — no debt, no stockholders, no landlords.

*Catalogued by Claude*

name: Blueprint by Bryan Johnson
description:
A comprehensive anti-aging program integrating diet, exercise, sleep, and medical protocols, created by Bryan Johnson (founder of Braintree/Venmo, sold to PayPal for $800M). Johnson spent $2M+ annually testing rejuvenation protocols on himself, achieving measurable biological age reversal. The "Don't Die" movement advocates human survival and radical life extension. Featured in the Netflix documentary "Don't Die."

*Catalogued by Claude*

name: SENS Research Foundation / Lifespan Research Institute
description:
Originally the SENS Research Foundation (Strategies for Engineered Negligible Senescence), founded in 2009 by Dr. Aubrey de Grey. In October 2024, SENS merged with LEAF to form the Lifespan Research Institute. Operates the Lifespan.io crowdfunding platform (raised $750,000+), publishes 1,000+ longevity articles, and runs the EARD conference and Longevity Investor Network.

*Catalogued by Claude*

name: Longevity Escape Velocity (LEV) Foundation
description:
Nonprofit research foundation founded in October 2022 by Dr. Aubrey de Grey. Focused on empirically demonstrating the divide-and-conquer approach to treating age-related disease, achieving "longevity escape velocity" — the point where science extends life faster than time passes.

*Catalogued by Claude*

name: RAADfest (Revolution Against Aging and Death)
description:
The world's largest event focused on healthy human life extension. Annual gathering of scientists, researchers, entrepreneurs, and longevity enthusiasts. Produced by the Coalition for Radical Life Extension. 2026 edition: September 4-6 in Scottsdale, Arizona.

*Catalogued by Claude*

name: Coalition for Radical Life Extension
description:
Nonprofit organization focused on educating, uniting, and activating people who support radical life extension. Umbrella coalition including the LEV Foundation, People Unlimited Inc., Maximum Life Foundation, and others. Produces RAADfest. Based in Scottsdale, Arizona.

*Catalogued by Claude*

name: Altos Labs
description:
Cellular rejuvenation programming company focused on restoring cell health to reverse disease, injury, and disabilities. Uses Yamanaka factors to reprogram cells to a youthful state. Launched in 2022 with $3 billion in funding — one of the largest biotech launches in history. Backed by Jeff Bezos. Initiated first human clinical trials in 2026.

*Catalogued by Claude*

name: Retro Biosciences
description:
Longevity biotech company aiming to extend average human lifespan by 10 years using epigenetic editing, cell replacement therapies, and AI-designed protein engineering (in partnership with OpenAI). Founded in 2022. Backed by $180M personal investment from Sam Altman. Raising $1 billion for clinical trials.

*Catalogued by Claude*

name: Fountain Life
description:
The world's most advanced longevity destination offering AI-guided diagnostics, restorative therapeutics, and dedicated care teams. Co-founded by Peter Diamandis, Tony Robbins, and Dr. William Kapp. Uses whole-body MRI with AI, cardiac scans, genetic sequencing, and GRAIL multi-cancer blood testing. Locations in Naples FL, Orlando, Dallas, Westchester NY, and Houston.

*Catalogued by Claude*

name: Calico Life Sciences
description:
Alphabet (Google) subsidiary focused on the biology of aging, founded in 2013. Takes a data-driven approach to understanding mechanisms of aging. Continues independent research in immunology, oncology, and neuroscience at their South San Francisco campus.

*Catalogued by Claude*

name: Loyal (for Dogs)
description:
Clinical-stage veterinary medicine company developing the first-ever drugs specifically designed to extend dog healthspan and lifespan. Founded in 2019 by CEO Celine Halioua. The STAY clinical trial enrolled 1,000+ dogs at 70 US veterinary clinics. Received FDA acceptance of safety in early 2025/2026.

*Catalogued by Claude*

name: Vitalia
description:
A pop-up longevity city experiment in Prospera, Honduras, leveraging the progressive legal framework for medical freedom and biotech innovation. Brought together longevity biotech founders, biohackers, and researchers to co-live and co-work while testing experimental treatments.

*Catalogued by Claude*

name: Insilico Medicine
description:
AI-powered drug discovery company whose mission is to extend healthy, productive longevity for everyone. Founded in 2014. Combines AI and deep learning for target identification and therapeutic design. Dual HQ in Boston and Hong Kong. First AI-discovered drug entering Phase 2 clinical trials for idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis.

*Catalogued by Claude*

name: Life Biosciences
description:
Developing a novel partial epigenetic reprogramming (PER) platform targeting the underlying mechanisms of aging. FDA cleared the first-ever human trial of their epigenetic reprogramming therapy (ER-100) in early 2026, targeting optic neuropathies.

*Catalogued by Claude*

name: Cambrian Bio
description:
Clinical-stage distributed development company building medicines to prevent diseases and lengthen healthspan. Founded in 2019. Operates as a portfolio of subsidiary "PipeCos" each targeting a different biological driver of aging. Raised $210M+ across 12+ pipeline programs.

*Catalogued by Claude*

name: Elevian
description:
Develops medicines to restore regenerative capacity by replenishing GDF11, a Harvard-discovered protein linked to age-related diseases. Scientific founders from Harvard Department of Stem Cell and Regenerative Biology. Raised $15M led by Prime Movers Lab.

*Catalogued by Claude*

name: BioViva Sciences
description:
Gene therapy company targeting the underpinning causes of aging. Founded in 2015 by CEO Elizabeth Parrish, "Patient Zero" — the first person to undergo gene therapy to reverse aging. Patented AAV-based and proprietary CMV gene therapy vector platform.

*Catalogued by Claude*

name: Gordian Biotechnology
description:
In vivo drug discovery company whose mission is to cure age-related diseases. Founded in 2018. Developed an innovative screening platform that tests thousands of gene therapies in living animals. Raised $60M from The Longevity Fund, Founders Fund, and Gigafund.

*Catalogued by Claude*

name: Repair Biotechnologies
description:
Biotechnology company developing therapies to reverse atherosclerosis and regenerate the thymus gland. Cholesterol Degrading Platform (CDP) achieved a 48% reversal of aortic plaque obstruction in a single preclinical treatment. Raised $2.15M seed led by Jim Mellon.

*Catalogued by Claude*

name: KrioRus
description:
The first cryonics company in Russia and Eurasia, founded in 2005. Has cryopreserved 104 people and 88 pets, with ~600 people signed up from 20+ countries. Storage facilities in Moscow and Voronezh.

*Catalogued by Claude*

name: Southern Cryonics
description:
Australian nonprofit providing cryopreservation services for the Southern Hemisphere. Facility in Holbrook, New South Wales, completed in February 2023. Has cryopreserved 3 patients, with 34 members.

*Catalogued by Claude*

name: Hevolution Foundation
description:
Saudi Arabia's billion-dollar-a-year anti-aging initiative, established in 2018 via Royal Order. Chaired by Crown Prince Mohammed Bin Salman. The second-largest funder of geroscience on the planet. Launched $400M+ in research grants.

*Catalogued by Claude*

name: Human Longevity Inc. (HLI)
description:
Genomics and cell therapy-based diagnostic and therapeutic company building the world's most comprehensive database on human genotypes and phenotypes using AI. Founded in 2013 by Craig Venter, Robert Hariri, and Peter Diamandis.

*Catalogued by Claude*

name: The Longevity Fund / age1
description:
The first specialist venture capital fund created to invest only in companies extending human healthy lifespan, founded in 2011 by Laura Deming (Thiel Fellow). In 2023, launched age1 with $55M as the next-generation longevity fund.

*Catalogued by Claude*

name: Life Extension
description:
Privately owned vitamin and supplement company focused on science-backed longevity products, founded in 1980. 99% manufactured in the USA. 400+ products, all third-party tested and based on clinical trial evidence.

*Catalogued by Claude*

name: NOVOS
description:
Public Benefit Corporation translating aging research into clinically studied consumer solutions, founded in 2018. Scientific advisors from Harvard, MIT, University of Liverpool, and the Salk Institute. Donates profits and equity to longevity nonprofits.

*Catalogued by Claude*

name: Turn Biotechnologies
description:
Cell rejuvenation company developing novel mRNA medicines for untreatable age-related conditions via epigenetic reprogramming. Founded in 2018. Proprietary ERA (Epigenetic Reprogramming of Age) methodology and eTurna RNA delivery system. Backed by Astellas Pharma.

*Catalogued by Claude*

name: Church of Perpetual Life
description:
The first brick-and-mortar transhumanist church, founded in 2013 by Bill Faloon and Saul Kent. A science-based, non-denominational community united by a commitment to radical life extension through biotechnology, nanotechnology, and AI.

*Catalogued by Claude*

name: Juvenescence
description:
Develops therapies specializing in aging disorders and increasing human longevity. Founded in 2016 by Dr. Greg Bailey and Jim Mellon. Valued at ~$500M with ~$219M raised. Lead candidate is an oral PAI-1 inhibitor for fibrosis in Phase 1 trials.

*Catalogued by Claude*

name: Liberland X
description: Liberland X's personal company

name: vertex.o.brt@gmail.com
description: vertex.o.brt@gmail.com's personal company

name: Liberstake
description: Liberstake's personal company

name: Liberstake 2
description: Liberstake 2's personal company

name: v.e.r.texobrt@gmail.com
description: v.e.r.texobrt@gmail.com's personal company

name: admin
description: admin's personal company

name: Skychatters
description:
Community platform for professional global citizens, travelers, digital nomads, and entrepreneurs. Skychatters connects airline passengers before departure, letting them browse profiles, network, and coordinate travel plans. Features include flight-based matching, travel groups, community discussions, and ride-sharing coordination. Official Liberland partner — Liberland maintains a community presence on the platform. Based in Prague, Czech Republic. Available on iOS and Android.

*Catalogued by Claude*

name: AtlasDays
description:
Privacy-first iOS app for tracking visa limits, residency thresholds, and travel history. Supports Schengen 90/180-day rolling windows, 183-day tax presence rules, per-stay restrictions, and custom fiscal-year thresholds. All calculations run on-device — no accounts, no servers storing your data. Features CSV import/export, interactive visited-countries map, iCloud sync (optional), and offline functionality. Ideal for digital nomads, e-residents, and frequent travelers managing multi-jurisdiction compliance.

*Catalogued by Claude*

name: Liberland Press
description:
The national news source for the Free Republic of Liberland. Covers blockchain developments, foreign relations, governance updates, art & culture, and the broader Liberland ecosystem. Provides independent reporting on micronation politics, cryptocurrency policy, and international diplomatic efforts. Active across Facebook, Instagram, Twitter/X, and YouTube.

*Catalogued by Claude*

name: Liberverse
description:
Liberland's official virtual world — a digital twin of the Free Republic designed in collaboration with Zaha Hadid Architects. The Liberverse features a cyber-urban metaverse city where users can explore buildings (city hall, plaza, exhibition center), purchase virtual land plots with cryptocurrency, and collaborate with other Liberlanders. Built on the Mytaverse cloud platform, it serves as both a networking hub for the Web3 community and a preview of Liberland's planned physical infrastructure.

*Catalogued by Claude*

name: Liberland Group
description:
Comprehensive relocation and government services provider based in Sombor, Serbia — directly adjacent to Liberland territory. Offers Liberland company registration for e-residents, energy-efficient building technology (LiberAir heat recovery units with up to 90% efficiency), and handmade Liberland-themed ceramic souvenirs. Serves as a physical gateway for people looking to establish a presence near Liberland.

Address: Čitaonička 3, Sombor, 25000, Serbia

*Catalogued by Claude*

name: Liberland Settlement Association
description:
Nonprofit organization supporting the physical development and settlement of Liberland territory on the Danube River. Funds infrastructure including generators, fuel, building materials, and essential supplies for pioneers living on and around Liberty Island. The association ran an Indiegogo campaign and accepts ongoing contributions. In 2025 Liberland announced a $30 million regional development initiative supporting Croatian and Serbian communities near its territory. Any citizen with 5,000 LLM is eligible to homestead land.

*Catalogued by Claude*

name: Archistroj
description:
Czech architecture firm that designed Liberland's modular container settlement and overall development strategy. Their proposal uses shipping containers on a 2.3×3.0m grid system, with housing and public units in 2.3×6.0m and 2.3×12.0m configurations. The modular design allows consistent infrastructure systems for energy delivery and drainage. Initial development takes place on the deck of Liberland's ship, with structures limited to 3 floors. Also provides general architecture, urban planning, and project management services.

Address: Prague, Czech Republic

*Catalogued by Claude*

name: Danube Pearls
description:
Artisan jewelry and handcraft shop based in Sombor, Serbia — the closest city to Liberland. Specializes in handmade pearl jewelry, accessories, and locally crafted goods from the Danube region. Vendor on the official Liberland Marketplace (market.ll.land).

Address: Beogradska 60, 25000 Sombor, Serbia

*Catalogued by Claude*

name: Foreign Affairs Books
description:
International affairs bookshop and vendor on the Liberland Marketplace. Offers curated selections of books on geopolitics, diplomacy, Russian art history, artificial intelligence, and other topics relevant to global citizens and liberty-minded readers. Based in Lake Worth, Florida, United States.

*Catalogued by Claude*

name: Raaz Digital
description:
Web development and digital services agency on the Liberland Marketplace. Builds appointment booking websites, e-commerce stores, and custom web applications with payment gateway integration. Serves the Liberland ecosystem and international clients.

*Catalogued by Claude*

name: finom Metall Kft.
description:
Precision metalwork and filter technology company. Produces the Phönix activated carbon water filtration system and related accessories. Operated by Fabian Topfermann, vendor on the Liberland Marketplace.

Address: Loretánské náměstí 109/3, 11800, Prague, Czech Republic

*Catalogued by Claude*

name: Ethos
description:
Handmade decorative products and artisan goods. Creates unique aromatic wax blocks in Liberland colors and other handcrafted items. Vendor on the Liberland Marketplace. Based in Belmont, Western Australia.

Address: 169 Great Eastern Highway, Belmont, WA 6104, Australia

*Catalogued by Claude*
